title: delivery: post body cloned per inbox from Vec<u8> in post_cache
description: In process_batch, the serialized activity body is stored as Vec<u8> in post_cache. On line 291, body.clone() is called to hand ownership to reqwest for every delivery target. For a popular post with many followers, the same payload is heap-allocated once per recipient. Storing the body as bytes::Bytes (an Arc-backed byte buffer) in post_cache would reduce this to a cheap reference-count increment per clone. reqwest::Body accepts Bytes directly.
close_reason: Accepted: ponytail comment. Clone cost negligible vs HTTP roundtrip.
